page.title: Starlite Festival 2026: Yacht Charter Tips for Marbella's Top Concert Series

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella Starlite Festival 2026 yacht charter. Find boats from €749, departure from Puerto Banús, and tips for combining concerts with a day at sea.
data.summary: A practical guide to combining a Starlite Festival 2026 concert with a yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Includes boat options, departure logistics, and how to make the most of your evening.
key_facts: Starlite Festival runs July–August 2026 in the Cantera de Nagüeles, Marbella.
Charter a boat from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) including skipper, drinks, and snacks.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, a 10-minute drive from the festival site.
Boat capacities range from 5 pax (Dubhe) to 12 pax (Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94).
Evening concert start times are typically 21:00–22:00; daytime charters finish by 18:00.
Tier B charters (Mangusta 80) from €4,719 for 4 hours include a free jet ski.
All prices include Spanish IVA (21%), fuel, insurance, and a licensed skipper.
Book early: Starlite weekends sell out boat availability from mid-May.

faq.q: How far is Puerto Banús from the Starlite Festival venue?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is about 10–15 minutes by taxi or private transfer from the Cantera de Nagüeles, where Starlite is held. The drive is roughly 6 km along the Golden Mile. Allow 20 minutes on concert nights due to traffic.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat just for the day and still attend the concert in the evening?
faq.a: Yes. Most day charters return to port by 18:00–19:00, leaving you ample time to shower, change, and head to the festival. Evening concerts typically start at 21:00 or 22:00.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a group of 10 attending Starlite?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 (11 pax) or the Fairline Targa 12m (10 pax) are ideal. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include all amenities. For a larger group, the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) offers extra space and a free jet ski.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Spanish IVA (21%) is also included. No hidden costs.

faq.q: Can we combine a Starlite concert with a sunset cruise?
faq.a: Absolutely. Sunset in Marbella in July–August is around 21:30. A 4-hour charter starting at 17:00 gives you a sunset cruise and returns by 21:00, just in time for a late concert start.

faq.q: Do you offer transfers from the port to the festival?
faq.a: Transfers are not included in the charter price, but we can arrange a private taxi or minibus on request. Contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

faq.q: Is there a minimum age for passengers?
faq.a: There is no minimum age, but children must be accompanied by an adult. Life jackets are available for all sizes. The skipper will brief everyone on safety before departure.

faq.q: How far in advance should I book for Starlite week?
faq.a: We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks ahead, especially for weekends with headline acts. Popular boats like the Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge can be fully booked by late June.

data.body_html:
Starlite Festival 2026 returns to Marbella from July to August, bringing world-class music to the Cantera de Nagüeles. For concert-goers who want to elevate their experience, combining a night of live music with a daytime or sunset yacht charter from Puerto Banús is a smart move. Here’s how to plan your Marbella Starlite Festival yacht charter — with concrete boat options, departure logistics, and practical tips.

Why charter a boat for Starlite?

A day on the water before the concert turns a single evening into a full Marbella experience. You can anchor off Cabopino for a swim, cruise past the Golden Mile, and return to port with time to change before the show. All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks — so you arrive relaxed and ready.

Boat options for Starlite 2026

We have 27 boats departing from Puerto Banús. For Starlite, these are the most popular choices:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) — from €749 for 2 hours. Ideal for couples or small groups.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — same price tier. Slightly more space.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) — from €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a free jet ski. Great for a party vibe.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) — pricing on request. Flybridge for panoramic views.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) — the flagship. Perfect for VIP treatment.

All prices include IVA, fuel, skipper, and refreshments. For other boats, contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote.

Timing your charter around the concert

Starlite concerts typically start at 21:00 or 22:00. A typical plan:

  • 10:00–14:00: Morning charter with lunch at a chiringuito (e.g., Cala del Faro).
  • 16:00–20:00: Sunset cruise, returning to port by 20:00.
  • 20:30: Taxi to Cantera de Nagüeles (10 min).
  • 21:00–23:00: Concert.

If you choose a 4-hour Tier B charter (Mangusta 80), you can leave at 15:00 and still be back by 19:00.

Departure from Puerto Banús

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s most famous marina. Parking is available (paid), or you can arrive by taxi. The port has restaurants, shops, and a supermarket if you need to pick up anything before boarding. The drive to the festival site is straightforward along the A-7 or through the Golden Mile.

What to bring to the festival after your charter

You’ll be on the boat in swimwear and light clothing. For the concert, pack a change of clothes, comfortable shoes (the venue has a mix of gravel and paved areas), and a light jacket — evenings can cool down with the sea breeze. Leave valuables on the boat if you’re returning to port after the show; we can store bags securely.

Booking tips for Starlite 2026

Starlite attracts international crowds, and boat availability tightens from mid-June. Book your Marbella Starlite Festival yacht charter at least 2–3 weeks in advance. Weekend dates with major headliners sell out first. If your preferred boat is unavailable, consider a smaller group boat like the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) — both are budget-friendly options.

Combine with other Marbella highlights

If you have a few days, consider a charter to Estepona or Sotogrande before the concert. The Mangusta 80 can reach Estepona in 30 minutes. Or anchor at Cala Cortés for a quieter swim spot. For a full itinerary, see our Starlite guide.

Frequently asked questions

How far is Puerto Banús from the Starlite Festival venue?

Puerto Banús is about 10–15 minutes by taxi or private transfer from the Cantera de Nagüeles, where Starlite is held. The drive is roughly 6 km along the Golden Mile. Allow 20 minutes on concert nights due to traffic.

Can I charter a boat just for the day and still attend the concert in the evening?

Yes. Most day charters return to port by 18:00–19:00, leaving you ample time to shower, change, and head to the festival. Evening concerts typically start at 21:00 or 22:00.

Which boat is best for a group of 10 attending Starlite?

The Azimut 39 (11 pax) or the Fairline Targa 12m (10 pax) are ideal. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include all amenities. For a larger group, the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) offers extra space and a free jet ski.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Spanish IVA (21%) is also included. No hidden costs.

Can we combine a Starlite concert with a sunset cruise?

Absolutely. Sunset in Marbella in July–August is around 21:30. A 4-hour charter starting at 17:00 gives you a sunset cruise and returns by 21:00, just in time for a late concert start.

Do you offer transfers from the port to the festival?

Transfers are not included in the charter price, but we can arrange a private taxi or minibus on request. Contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

Is there a minimum age for passengers?

There is no minimum age, but children must be accompanied by an adult. Life jackets are available for all sizes. The skipper will brief everyone on safety before departure.

How far in advance should I book for Starlite week?

We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks ahead, especially for weekends with headline acts. Popular boats like the Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge can be fully booked by late June.
